```{r}
# ############################################################################
#  SLIDES 8 / 9 / 10 — variable-combination experiments (LPA)
#  Runs each combination, prints K, sizes, BIC pattern, and ARI vs Rome IV.
#  Combinations:
#    Slide 8  : Wide + EMA + GI    (= our Experiment D; LPA version)
#    Slide 9  : Wide + EOD + GI
#    Slide 10 : Wide + EOD
#  (bonus)    : Wide + EOD + EMA   (= our Experiment B)
#
#  EOD = the 20 PANAS daily-diary adjectives, columns end in "_diary".
#  GI  = 4 episode distress items.
# ############################################################################

pkgs <- c("dplyr","mclust")
for (p in pkgs) if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly=TRUE))
  install.packages(p, repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")
library(dplyr); library(mclust)

base_path <- "/Users/seobeomjin/Desktop/SURA IBS Clustering/"
wide    <- read.csv(paste0(base_path, "wide_assign.csv"))
ema     <- read.csv(paste0(base_path, "EMA_B1_SURA.csv"))
eod     <- read.csv(paste0(base_path, "EOD_B1_SURA.csv"))
episode <- read.csv(paste0(base_path, "episode_B1_SURA.csv"))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Build per-patient feature blocks (one row per PID each)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# (1) WIDE: 3 baseline scales
wide_block <- wide %>% select(PID, BSI_Total_T1, SSS_Sum_T1_Recode, VSI_Sum_T1)

# (2) EMA: mean + SD of 3 signals  (>=10 responses)
ema_block <- ema %>% group_by(PID) %>% filter(n() >= 10) %>%
  summarise(neg_mean=mean(negative_beep,na.rm=TRUE), neg_sd=sd(negative_beep,na.rm=TRUE),
            stress_mean=mean(stress,na.rm=TRUE),     stress_sd=sd(stress,na.rm=TRUE),
            distress_mean=mean(distress,na.rm=TRUE), distress_sd=sd(distress,na.rm=TRUE),
            .groups="drop")

# (3) EOD: the 20 PANAS daily-diary AFFECT adjectives, per-patient mean.
#     IMPORTANT: we list the 20 explicitly. Auto-detecting "_diary" columns is
#     unsafe because the EOD file also contains social-contact items
#     (interact_who_diary is TEXT, interact_num/social_interact are counts,
#     not affect) — including them produced all-NA columns and N=0.
panas_20 <- c("lively_diary","sad_diary","happy_diary","on_edge_diary",
              "at_ease_diary","hostile_diary","grateful_diary","full_of_pep_diary",
              "depressed_diary","pleased_diary","nervous_diary","calm_diary",
              "resentful_diary","lonely_diary","energetic_diary","unhappy_diary",
              "cheerful_diary","tense_diary","relaxed_diary","angry_diary")

# the diary items live in the EOD file; keep only the 20 that are actually present
diary_cols <- panas_20[panas_20 %in% names(eod)]
cat("EOD: using", length(diary_cols), "PANAS affect adjectives (of 20 expected)\n")
if (length(diary_cols) < 15)
  stop("Fewer than 15 PANAS columns found in EOD — check column names.")

# coerce to numeric defensively (some may import as character), then mean
eod_block <- eod %>% group_by(PID) %>% filter(n() >= 3) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(diary_cols), ~mean(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x)), na.rm=TRUE)),
            .groups="drop")

# (4) GI: 4 episode distress/relief items (>=3 episodes); per-patient mean.
#     All four are numeric rating scales (verified in the dictionary):
#     distress before / while / intensely during a BM, and relief after.
gi_vars <- c("distress_leading_ep","distress_sitting_ep","distress_intense_ep","bm_relief")
gi_vars <- gi_vars[gi_vars %in% names(episode)]      # keep those present
cat("GI: using", length(gi_vars), "episode items:", paste(gi_vars, collapse=", "), "\n\n")
gi_block <- episode %>% group_by(PID) %>% filter(n() >= 3) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(gi_vars), ~mean(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x)), na.rm=TRUE)),
            .groups="drop")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  DIAGNOSTIC: show the size of each feature block before combining
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n########## BLOCK DIAGNOSTICS ##########\n")
cat(sprintf("wide_block: %d patients, %d vars\n", nrow(wide_block), ncol(wide_block)-1))
cat(sprintf("ema_block : %d patients, %d vars\n", nrow(ema_block),  ncol(ema_block)-1))
cat(sprintf("eod_block : %d patients, %d vars  (%s)\n", nrow(eod_block), ncol(eod_block)-1,
            paste(head(setdiff(names(eod_block),"PID"),3), collapse=", ")))
cat(sprintf("gi_block  : %d patients, %d vars  (%s)\n", nrow(gi_block), ncol(gi_block)-1,
            paste(setdiff(names(gi_block),"PID"), collapse=", ")))

# how many patients survive each overlap?
ov <- function(...) length(Reduce(intersect, list(...)))
cat(sprintf("\nOverlap (common PIDs):\n"))
cat(sprintf("  wide+ema+gi : %d\n", ov(wide_block$PID, ema_block$PID, gi_block$PID)))
cat(sprintf("  wide+eod+gi : %d   <- slide 9 (was failing)\n",
            ov(wide_block$PID, eod_block$PID, gi_block$PID)))
cat(sprintf("  wide+eod    : %d\n", ov(wide_block$PID, eod_block$PID)))
cat(sprintf("  wide+eod+ema: %d\n", ov(wide_block$PID, eod_block$PID, ema_block$PID)))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Helper: join chosen blocks, run LPA, print a clean result line
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_combo <- function(blocks, label) {
  # blocks = list of data frames each keyed by PID
  d <- Reduce(function(a,b) inner_join(a,b,by="PID"), blocks)
  vars <- setdiff(names(d), "PID")

  # --- guard: drop any column that is entirely NA (would wipe all rows) ---
  all_na <- vars[sapply(d[, vars], function(col) all(is.na(col)))]
  if (length(all_na) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  !! dropping %d all-NA column(s): %s\n",
                length(all_na), paste(all_na, collapse=", ")))
    d <- d[, setdiff(names(d), all_na)]
    vars <- setdiff(vars, all_na)
  }
  d <- na.omit(d)

  cat(sprintf("\n=== %s ===\n", label))
  cat(sprintf("  N = %d   variables = %d\n", nrow(d), length(vars)))

  # --- safety check 1: enough patients relative to variables? ---
  if (nrow(d) < length(vars) + 5) {
    cat(sprintf("  !! N (%d) too small for %d variables — LPA needs N >> p.\n",
                nrow(d), length(vars)))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # --- safety check 2: drop zero/near-zero variance columns (break scale()) ---
  sds <- sapply(d[, vars], sd, na.rm=TRUE)
  bad <- names(sds)[is.na(sds) | sds < 1e-8]
  if (length(bad) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf("  !! dropping %d zero-variance variable(s): %s\n",
                length(bad), paste(bad, collapse=", ")))
    vars <- setdiff(vars, bad)
  }

  X <- scale(d[, vars])
  # --- safety check 3: any remaining NA/NaN after scaling? ---
  if (any(!is.finite(X))) {
    cat("  !! non-finite values remain after scaling — cannot run LPA.\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  set.seed(42)
  m <- tryCatch(Mclust(X, G=2:6), error=function(e){
    cat("  !! mclust failed:", conditionMessage(e), "\n"); NULL })
  if (is.null(m)) return(invisible(NULL))

  bic <- sapply(2:6, function(k) round(max(m$BIC[as.character(k),], na.rm=TRUE),1))
  mono <- all(diff(bic[which.max(bic):length(bic)]) < 0)
  rome <- wide$IBS_type[match(d$PID, wide$PID)]
  ari  <- adjustedRandIndex(m$classification, rome)

  cat(sprintf("  K = %d   sizes: %s\n", m$G, paste(table(m$classification), collapse=" / ")))
  cat(sprintf("  BIC (K2..K6): %s   %s\n", paste(bic, collapse=" "),
              if (m$G==2 && mono) "[robust K=2, monotonic]" else "[check pattern]"))
  cat(sprintf("  ARI vs Rome IV = %.3f  (near 0 = independent of bowel-habit type)\n", ari))
  invisible(m)
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#  RUN THE COMBINATIONS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("############ SLIDE 8: Wide + EMA + GI ############")
run_combo(list(wide_block, ema_block, gi_block), "Wide + EMA + GI")

cat("\n\n############ SLIDE 9: Wide + EOD + GI ############")
run_combo(list(wide_block, eod_block, gi_block), "Wide + EOD + GI")

cat("\n\n############ SLIDE 10: Wide + EOD ############")
run_combo(list(wide_block, eod_block), "Wide + EOD")

cat("\n\n############ BONUS: Wide + EOD + EMA (Experiment B) ############")
run_combo(list(wide_block, eod_block, ema_block), "Wide + EOD + EMA")

cat("\n\nDONE. Record N, K, sizes, BIC pattern, and ARI for each slide.\n")
```
